items.headline: Bullish Sentiment Persists Amid Inflation Concerns and Sector Rotation
items.summary: The S&P 500 has extended its rally for an eighth consecutive week, driven by strong dip-buying and minimal drawdowns, indicating persistent bullish sentiment. However, this optimism is tempered by underlying concerns, including accelerating inflation, potential market impact from large upcoming IPOs like SpaceX, and waning oil inventories. Consumer sentiment in May plummeted to a record low due to worries over cost of living and high gasoline prices. A significant tech rotation was observed in global markets, with Japan's Nikkei surging while NVIDIA faced profit-taking. US markets were closed on Monday, May 25, for Memorial Day, with economic data releases and major earnings anticipated later in the week.

items.headline: D-Wave Quantum Inc. surges on government funding news
items.summary: Shares surged following news of a proposed $100 million CHIPS Act funding package from the U.S. Department of Commerce, validating the company's quantum computing technology and growth prospects.

items.headline: Rigetti Computing Inc. climbs amid quantum computing optimism
items.summary: The quantum computing specialist experienced a significant surge, benefiting from strong investor optimism and being named a top beneficiary of the U.S. government's quantum computing investment initiative.

items.headline: GlobalFoundries Inc. rises on advanced foundry growth prospects
items.summary: Stock trended up amid investor optimism on advanced foundry growth, driven by a prospective $375 million CHIPS Act award and a focus on quantum technology solutions.

items.headline: Qualcomm ($QCOM) surges as AI aspirations gain momentum
items.summary: Shares surged as Wall Street reassessed the company's value amidst its pivotal role in the AI sector, reaching an all-time high.

items.headline: Apple Inc. sees gains driven by strong demand and AI outlook
items.summary: Apple shares rose, with analysts citing strong iPhone demand, a large installed base, and potential future growth from Services and AI/spatial computing.

items.headline: NVIDIA Corp. dips due to profit-taking despite strong earnings
items.summary: Shares dipped due to profit-taking and investor expectations for even higher revenue and earnings after a blockbuster earnings report.

items.headline: enGene Therapeutics Inc. records a decline
items.summary: enGene Therapeutics Inc. experienced a decline in its stock price.

items.headline: Bitcoin Depot Inc. plunges amid bankruptcy and delisting news
items.summary: The company is facing significant challenges, including a voluntary Chapter 11 bankruptcy filing on May 18, 2026, and an upcoming Nasdaq delisting.

items.headline: Fed Under New Leadership Amidst Inflationary Pressures
items.summary: Kevin Warsh was sworn in as the new Federal Reserve Chair on May 22, 2026, inheriting an economy grappling with a 3.75% federal funds rate and elevated core PCE inflation. The FOMC maintained its benchmark rates at 3.50% to 3.75% in April. Discussions are ongoing regarding potential rate hikes in response to persistent inflation, partly fueled by geopolitical tensions and higher energy prices. Upcoming US inflation data this week will be closely watched, alongside the Q1 US Real GDP data expected on Thursday, May 28.

items.headline: Consumer Sentiment Plunges to Record Low
items.summary: The University of Michigan's consumer sentiment index dropped to an all-time low of 44.8 in May 2026. This significant decline, the lowest since 1952, is primarily attributed to heightened concerns over the cost of living and rising gasoline prices. Wholesale inflation also hit a multiyear high in April, with manufacturing input prices surging in May, suggesting ongoing inflationary pressures.

items.headline: AI Boom Continues to Drive Sector Performance and Earnings
items.summary: The technology sector has soared 18.1% year-to-date, largely propelled by optimism surrounding the artificial intelligence boom. Key companies like Apple are seeing growth driven by AI and services, while Qualcomm's stock surged as it strengthens its role in the AI sector. Upcoming earnings reports for the week of May 25-29, 2026, from major tech and retail companies, including HP Inc., Salesforce, Dell Technologies, and Costco Wholesale, will provide further insights into AI monetization strategies and broader market trends.

items.headline: Sector Rotation and Geopolitical Tensions Shape Market Trends
items.summary: Investors are actively rotating into small and microcap stocks, REITs, consumer cyclical, and value equities, moving away from large caps, certain commodities, and growth stocks. Energy remains a top-performing sector year-to-date, though it saw some cooling off recently, influenced by elevated oil prices stemming from the ongoing Iran conflict. The US-Israeli war with Iran has significantly impacted energy markets, pushing WTI crude prices to around $96.60 a barrel and contributing to inflationary pressures.
